title: Thirunakkara

infobox.value: is a [Hindu](/wiki/Hindu "Hindu") place of worship in [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam"), India, dedicated to [Shiva](/wiki/Shiva "Shiva"). It is one of the [108 revered Shivalayas](/wiki/108_Shiva_Temples "108 Shiva Temples") in [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"). The temple is believed to be built by [Maharaja](/wiki/Maharaja "Maharaja") of [Thekkumkur](/wiki/Thekkumkur "Thekkumkur") about 500 years ago. It also preserves a number of sculptures and murals of various [Hindu deities](/wiki/Hindu_deities "Hindu deities"). A common belief is that the idol of [Shiva](/wiki/Shiva "Shiva") here is installed by [Parashurama](/wiki/Parashurama "Parashurama"). The Thekkumkur royal family considered the idol as their *paradevatha* ([tutelary deity](/wiki/Tutelary_deity "Tutelary deity")) in the form of *Thirunakkara thevar*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

([Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam_language "Malayalam language"): തിരുനക്കര, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Malayalam "Romanization of Malayalam"): *tirunakkara*) is a locality of [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam") city in [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"),[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) best known for the temple.

["Thirunakkara Mahadevar Temple, Kottayam - Kerala Tourism"](https://www.keralatourism.org/destination/thirunakkara-temple-kottayam/466). *www.keralatourism.com*. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Thirunakkara](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara&redirect=no "Thirunakkara"))

Temple of Lord Shiva in Kerala, India

**Thirunakkara Mahadevar Temple** is a [Hindu](/wiki/Hindu "Hindu") place of worship in [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam"), India, dedicated to [Shiva](/wiki/Shiva "Shiva"). It is one of the [108 revered Shivalayas](/wiki/108_Shiva_Temples "108 Shiva Temples") in [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"). The temple is believed to be built by [Maharaja](/wiki/Maharaja "Maharaja") of [Thekkumkur](/wiki/Thekkumkur "Thekkumkur") about 500 years ago. It also preserves a number of sculptures and murals of various [Hindu deities](/wiki/Hindu_deities "Hindu deities"). A common belief is that the idol of [Shiva](/wiki/Shiva "Shiva") here is installed by [Parashurama](/wiki/Parashurama "Parashurama"). The Thekkumkur royal family considered the idol as their *paradevatha* ([tutelary deity](/wiki/Tutelary_deity "Tutelary deity")) in the form of *Thirunakkara thevar*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

A King of Thekkumkoor royal dynasty was a big devotee of [Thrissur Vadakkumnathan](/wiki/Vadakkumnathan_Temple "Vadakkumnathan Temple"). Though there was a large Shiva temple called [Thalikotta Temple](/wiki/Thalikotta_Mahadeva_Temple "Thalikotta Mahadeva Temple") near his palace, where he regularly used to visit, he could not be happy without a visit to Vadakkumnathan Temple once in a month. Despite all problems faced, he used to visit Vadakkumnathan Temple throughout his life. But, as years passed, the King became aged, and he could no longer travel for long distance. Thus he was sunk in grief. Lord Shiva appeared before him and consoled him saying that he would appear in a location where his great devotee can travel easily. Thus the King was pleased.

During his return, the King also visited the famous [Shiva temple](/wiki/Vaikom_Mahadeva_Temple "Vaikom Mahadeva Temple") at [Vaikom](/wiki/Vaikom "Vaikom"). There he met with a poor Brahmin, named Perepparambu Namboothiri, who was conducting his bhajanam due to financial difficulties. The King told him that he would provide all necessities for getting money, and took him to his palace. That night, the King had a dream, in which Lord Shiva appeared before him, and saying that he would appear in Nakkarakkunnu - a small hill located a few miles away from the palace area - as a Swayambhoo lingam, and there would be an idol of [Nandi](/wiki/Nandi_(mythology) "Nandi (mythology)"), his vehicle in front of him, and a white [ixora](/wiki/Ixora "Ixora") plant on his backside towards the left. The King became very much happy.

Nakkarakkunnu, the place where the present temple exists, was a huge forest area then. Wild animals roamed there without any problem. No one wanted to settle there, even if it was given freely. There was a monastery called Swamiyar Madom, titled *Thrikkaikkattu Madom*, on the north-east side of the temple. On the day after the King got the darshan of Lord Shiva, two servants from the Swamiyar Madom - Changazhissery Moothathu and Punnassery Moothathu - went to collect wood and fire for a homam (fire ritual). They saw a stone there and scraped their sickle on it, but suddenly it started bleeding. They soon realised that it was a Swayambhoo Shivalingam. The news spread like a bush fire, and it also reached the ears of the King, who could not control his emotions after hearing the news. The King came to the location where the lingam was installed, and prostrated before it. He also found that there was an idol of Nandi in front of it, and a white ixora plant on its backside towards the left. After that, he made the biggest temple in his territory for his favourite deity, with all the major components of a mahakshetra (major temple). Tharananelloor Nammbothiri became the head priest of the temple, and Perepparambu Namboothiri was installed as the senior priest. The King got blessed by visiting the temple, and lived his life happily thereafter.

## Brahmarakshas

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Brahmarakshas")]

There is an interesting story behind the *Brahma Rakshas*. One person called *Moose* was a great friend of the king. The king was not known for his beauty but his friend Moose was very handsome. The queen fell in love with this friend knowing which the king ordered his servants to kill Moose. Instead of killing him, the King’s servants killed the junior priest of the temple (*keezh santhi*). The wife of the priest became a Brahma Rakshas and started seeking revenge. So the king built a temple for her. For a long time afterwards, women do not prefer to enter this temple.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Architecture")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/Kottayam_Mural_City_Near_Gandhi_Statue%2C_Thirunakara_Maidanam.JPG/250px-Kottayam_Mural_City_Near_Gandhi_Statue%2C_Thirunakara_Maidanam.JPG)](/wiki/File:Kottayam_Mural_City_Near_Gandhi_Statue,_Thirunakara_Maidanam.JPG)

A mural painting near Thirunakkara temple

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/43/Thirunakkara_Mahadeva_temple_gopurams.jpg/250px-Thirunakkara_Mahadeva_temple_gopurams.jpg)](/wiki/File:Thirunakkara_Mahadeva_temple_gopurams.jpg)

Gupurams at the entrance

The temple situated in the heart of Kottayam city is built in the traditional architectural styles of Kerala. The first entrance to the temple includes a small temple for lord [Ganapathi](/wiki/Ganapathi "Ganapathi") which was built recently. After crossing the entrance, we could see the big ground leading to the holy steps of the temple. There is a [banyan](/wiki/Banyan "Banyan") tree on steps which is considered sacred by the devotees. The *Aanakottil* and *Kodimaram* is placed close to the eastern entrance. The Kodimaram built in 1960 is only 42 feet long. To the side of Kodimaram lies the *Balikkalpuras*. Like that in [Guruvayoor](/wiki/Guruvayoor "Guruvayoor") temple, one could clearly see the idol of chief deity from the main entrance itself.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The whole temple complex occupies about 4 acres (1.6 ha) of land which is rich in many trees and plants. One of the largest *[Koothambalams](/wiki/Koothambalam "Koothambalam")* in Kerala is in this temple situated in the southeast corner. The temple has shrines for various sub deities in different locations. The southeast entrance consists of the shrines of lord [Ayyappa](/wiki/Ayyappa "Ayyappa") and Ganesha. *Naga pratishtas* are also close to these shrines. In the northwest section, there is a *Chethi* ([Jungle flame](/wiki/Ixora_coccinea "Ixora coccinea")) flower, commonly seen in many [Hindu](/wiki/Hindu "Hindu") shrines across Kerala. The eastern section includes the shrines of lord [Subhramaniya](/wiki/Muruga "Muruga") and [Durga](/wiki/Durga "Durga") and the northeast section has the installation of *Brahmarakshas* which is according to the myths the soul of a priest assassinated inside the temple.

### Sreekovil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Sreekovil")]

The two storeyed square shaped *sreekovil* of temple is very attractive and it is adorned by a golden [Finial](/wiki/Finial "Finial"). The sreekovil includes three separate rooms, one in the west is [Garbhagriha](/wiki/Garbhagriha "Garbhagriha") which has the [Shiva linga](/wiki/Shiva_linga "Shiva linga") idol installed. An idol of [Parvathi](/wiki/Parvathi "Parvathi") devi made up of [Panchaloha](/wiki/Panchaloha "Panchaloha") is also installed next to it. This complex has a number of murals depicting the stories of Shiva and [Dashavathara](/wiki/Dashavathara "Dashavathara"). Full circumambulation of the temple is not allowed because it is a Shiva shrine. It is said that Lord Shiva resides in his most peaceful form here, which is considered to be the reason for the prosperity of Kottayam.

## Festival

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Festival")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/Kottayam_Thirunakkara_temple_2023_festival_02.jpg/250px-Kottayam_Thirunakkara_temple_2023_festival_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kottayam_Thirunakkara_temple_2023_festival_02.jpg)

Festival in 2023

The temple hosts an annual ten-day festival ending with the [aaraattu](/wiki/Aaraattu "Aaraattu") ritual of dipping an idol of a deity into a river or [temple tank](/wiki/Temple_tank "Temple tank"), which includes a procession of nine decorated elephants.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The festival is a celebration of Shiva's wedding to [Parvati](/wiki/Parvati "Parvati") and is dedicated to Shiva.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) During the festival, traditional Keralan dances such as [Mayilattom](/wiki/Mayilattom "Mayilattom") and [Velakali](/wiki/Velakali "Velakali") are performed within the temple premises in the evening. Another major attraction is the [Kathakali](/wiki/Kathakali "Kathakali") performances during the festival.

## Subordinate deities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Subordinate deities")]

* [Ganapathi](/wiki/Ganapathi "Ganapathi")
* [Ayyappan](/wiki/Ayyappan "Ayyappan")
* [Subramanyan](/wiki/Subramanyan "Subramanyan")
* [Naga](/wiki/N%C4%81ga "Nāga")
* Vadakkumnathan (Another manifestation of Shiva, presiding deity at the Vadakkumnathan Temple in Thrissur)
* [Durga](/wiki/Durga "Durga")
* Brahmarakshas

## Thirunakkara

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thirunakkara_Sree_Mahadevar_Temple&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Thirunakkara")]

**Thirunakkara** ([Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam_language "Malayalam language"): തിരുനക്കര, [romanized](/wiki/Romanization_of_Malayalam "Romanization of Malayalam"): *tirunakkara*) is a locality of [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam") city in [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"),[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) best known for the temple.

## External links
